title: The Adventure of the Dying Detective
synopsis: Mrs. Hudson fetches Watson because Holmes appears to be dying of a rare Asiatic disease contracted during a case in Rotherhithe. Holmes seems delirious, refuses Watson's medical attention, and insists on summoning a specialist named Culverton Smith. Watson is mystified by Holmes's erratic behaviour and curious instructions.
solution: Holmes has been feigning illness to lure Culverton Smith, who had murdered his nephew Victor Savage with a poisoned device concealed in a box; Holmes tricks Smith into a confession by allowing him to believe Holmes is dying, while Watson and Inspector Morton are hidden in the room to make the arrest.
notable_elements: Holmes fasts for three days and uses cosmetics — belladonna, rouge, and beeswax — to simulate a dying man
He deliberately keeps Watson ignorant because Watson cannot dissimulate convincingly
The trap springs when Smith gloats over Holmes's presumed dying state and confesses
Features detailed description of Holmes's Baker Street domestic habits and Mrs. Hudson's loyalty
